ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who took the test */,
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test type */,
  "fecha_prueba" TEXT /* Date the test was taken */,
  "tipo_prueba" TEXT /* Type of test taken */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test */,
  "test_location" TEXT /* Location where the test was conducted */,
  "test_performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the health professional who performed the test */,
  "test_result_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the test result */,
  "test_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the test */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_type_id") REFERENCES test_types ("test_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_type" TEXT /* Type of the department */,
  "department_head" TEXT /* Name of the department head */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the department */,
  "department_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the department */,
  "department_address" TEXT /* Address of the department */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_types" (
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test type */,
  "test_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the test type */,
  "test_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the test type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table where the action occurred */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record affected */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
